title: MANGA Shipping in AD SIRP other manga
display_title: MANGA Shipping in AD SIRP other manga
wikitext:
[]: [MANGA] Shipping in AD/SIRP/other manga.             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jul 12, 2010 8:31 pm Convince me why I should like Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Jul 12, 2010 8:50 pm You make a good point, AuraTwilight. But then again, almost anything is better then Angelic Days.

[]: Except it needs moar incest. Or less incest. I forget which.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Mon Jul 12, 2010 9:22 pm child of Lilith wrote: You make a good point, AuraTwilight. But then again, almost anything is better then Angelic Days.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon Jul 12, 2010 9:23 pm I don't know if I'd go quite that far, Mari.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Jul 12, 2010 9:37 pm Hell, the last two pages of this thread are better then Angelic Days.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Mon Jul 12, 2010 10:09 pm But are they better than Shinji Ikari Raising Project?

[]: "I didn't know! I didn't know!"             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Mon Jul 12, 2010 10:12 pm Lucretius wrote: But are they better than Shinji Ikari Raising Project?

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Jul 12, 2010 10:18 pm Lucretius wrote: But are they better than Shinji Ikari Raising Project?

[]: Delicious Asuka is delicious.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Mon Jul 12, 2010 10:24 pm child of Lilith wrote: I’ve never read it, so I don’t know. But probably.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Jul 12, 2010 10:27 pm Is there more then just fan service? Because if that’s all there is then its not for me, sorry.

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Mon Jul 12, 2010 10:30 pm child of Lilith wrote: Is there more then just fan service? Because if that’s all there is then its not for me, sorry.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Jul 12, 2010 10:35 pm I hate shipping, so its definitely not for me then.

[]: Topic? We had a topic?             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Mon Jul 12, 2010 10:37 pm child of Lilith wrote: I hate shipping, so its definitely not for me then.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Jul 12, 2010 10:52 pm I like what I like and hate what I hate. To be clear, I don’t hate shipping so much as I hate the atmosphere it creates. Its really hard to get a good discussion going when everyone is to busy fighting over who should be with who.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Tue Jul 20, 2010 4:11 pm Sorry to bump this, because I hated Angelic Days, and I don't really plan to read Shinji Ikari Raising Project, but I at least liked how the art looked like the original show. But he ends up with Rei in SIRP right? Cause I don't want to waste my time if that is the case.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Jul 20, 2010 4:21 pm Last I checked, SIRP kept Shinji in an eternal state of indecisiveness, though the main focus of the story seems to be Rei. There's also, if I remember right, a scene where Asuka is about to tell him she likes him only to realize that he stopped paying attention a few minutes earlier and is trying to look up her kimono. For some reason the "wait, why did I ever like this guy, again?" instinct doesn't activate, and the status quo goes on. I've heard Angelic Days has better storytelling, at least, but it doesn't really seem like it'd be much of an accomplishment considering the competition. And AD's artwork made it impossible for me to read, anyway.

[]: I like it. You should too.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Tue Jul 20, 2010 6:23 pm This is the entire plot of SIRP: "Will Shinji end up with Rei or Asuka?" Of course, if that question is answered and he "ends up" with someone, the manga is over. Thus, the Status Quo Is God and must be upheld at all costs. Whenever Rei or Asuka is close to confessing her love to Shinji, something happens to interrupt them. If nothing interrupts them, something happens to make Shinji conveniently forget about it. It's a fun, lighthearted, fanservicey romp with clever writing and plenty of humor. But it can also be genuinely heartwarming. Just don't expect a deep plot or complex character development. It's more or less an Evangelion soap-opera.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Tue Jul 20, 2010 10:18 pm Which I don't mind but I just heard it is heavy on the Rei/Shinji and not the Asuka/Shinji.

[]: How about Rei/Asuka?             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Wed Jul 21, 2010 4:16 pm Lonecow12 wrote: Which I don't mind but I just heard it is heavy on the Rei/Shinji and not the Asuka/Shinji.

[]: I kinda feel GoS2 and SIRP games = (not > ) their manga.             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 22, 2010 1:35 am AD/GoS2 is good for a plot-based story of "will Shinji choose Asuka or RiAO?" with Asuka winning out. I personally love AD because all the characters are likeable and the artwork improves with time (volumes 3 to 6 is noticable). SIRP is good for fanservice-style "Who will Shinji choose?" story. Despite apperances by Kaworu and Mana--despite Asuka having kissed Shinji (Rei accidently got to him first tho)--RiAO/Shinji is winning out so far, at Vol10. I'd like SIRP better if it had Rei-not RiAO-& more plot.
